data: This is where character details will go, details that we’ve learned about the all of the characters that we’ve learned about. Divided between player characters (that’s you!) and non-player characters.

data.relationships#Relationships: Close friends with Syren, grew up together in the High Court

data.motivations#Motivations: Hunting for her half-sister Resentful towards mother for leaving Resentful towards father for general dickishness
data.relationships#Relationships: Close friends with Aelyra, grew up together in the High Court

data.character-traits#Character Traits: Self-Assured Standoffish and independent Of questionable morality (but “not a monster” as she says) Might be crazy with the whole duck thing, maybe?
data.marzipan#Marzipan: Marzipan is an invisible, potentially imaginary Duck to whom Vesper believes she owes her life after an incident in Ratwhistle during her childhood that nearly took her life. This duck provides much needed companionship to Vesper, who travels alone, and Vesper frequently consults her feathered friend for advice on risky situations. Is Marzipan real? Time will tell. Time… Will… Tell…

data: This is where all of the setting details will go, details that we’ve learned about the places we’ve come up with, where they are, the people and places there, etc. Organized by country.

The story starts in the mid-sized trade town of Norville. It’s a wet, cold, rainy spring day, some time between 2 and 4 pm, maybe 3 pm, who’s to say, live your own truth or whatever. Four adventurers are coincidentally drawn to Norville for one reason or another.
Elewyn, the mysterious druid (NOT cleric, thank you very much) was drawn to Norville by the wind, which told her that
